title: croton cerinodentatus
intro: Croton cerinodentatus, also known as the "Wild Potato", is a medicinal plant species native to tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its edible tubers and various medicinal properties. The tubers of Croton cerinodentatus contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating digestive problems. In herbal preparations, Croton cerinodentatus is often used in the form of decoctions or infusions, as well as being dried and powdered for use in traditional medicine.
